name: scientific-research
title: Scientific & Academic Research
description: Adapts the harness to produce scientific deliverables — research manuscripts and literature reviews — where the failure mode is an uncited claim, a non-reproducible method, or a conclusion that overreaches the results, not a failing build.

stages.label: Frame
stages.purpose: Define the research question, the scope, and the prior-art positioning — what is known, what gap this work addresses.

stages.label: Design
stages.purpose: State the hypothesis, the methods, and the analysis plan before any result is written. The plan the draft must follow.

stages.label: Draft
stages.purpose: Write the manuscript: methods, results, narrative — each claim cited, each conclusion traceable to a result.

stages.label: Review breadth
stages.purpose: Scan every section for completeness and internal consistency; flag missing sections, uncited claims, results without analysis.

stages.label: Review depth
stages.purpose: Run the gate verdicts: reproducibility, citation integrity, statistical validity, claim substantiation, novelty & scope.

stages.label: Feedback
stages.purpose: Evaluate reviewer challenges, reconcile conclusions with results, finalize limitations.

gates.name: Reproducibility
gates.question: Are the methods detailed enough — materials, procedure, parameters, analysis — for an independent group to replicate the study?

gates.name: Citation Integrity
gates.question: Does every claim trace to a cited reference, with no invented or uncited references — every inline citation resolved and every reference cited?

gates.name: Statistical Validity
gates.question: Are the statistical tests appropriate for the data, and are effect sizes and uncertainty (CIs, error, p-values) reported rather than bare point estimates?

gates.name: Claim Substantiation
gates.question: Do the conclusions match the results without overreach — no causal language from correlational data, no generalization beyond the sample?

gates.name: Novelty & Scope
gates.question: Is the contribution stated explicitly and is prior work positioned, so the reader can see what is new and where it sits in the literature?
